name: Germany

visas.name: Skilled Immigration Act Visa (Fachkräfteeinwanderungsgesetz)
visas.description: Germany's main work visa for qualified professionals from outside the EU. Covers both academic qualifications and vocational training. No job offer required if you use the Chancenkarte.
requirements: Job offer in a related field (or Chancenkarte for job search)

visas.name: Chancenkarte (Opportunity Card)
visas.description: Points-based job-seeker visa introduced in 2024. Allows you to live in Germany for up to 1 year while looking for work. You need 6 points across qualification, language, experience, age, and previous Germany ties.
requirements: Recognised university degree OR vocational qualification
At least 6 points on the points system
visas.maxDuration: 1 year (can convert to work visa once job found)

visas.name: EU Blue Card (Blaue Karte EU)
visas.description: Fast-track work permit for highly qualified non-EU workers. Requires a university degree and a salary above a set threshold. Leads to permanent residence faster than standard routes.
requirements: Job offer with salary ≥ €45,300/year (€41,042 for shortage occupations in 2024)
visas.leadsTo: Niederlassungserlaubnis after 27 months (21 months with B1 German)

visas.name: Family Reunification (Familiennachzug)
visas.description: Visa for spouses, children, and in some cases parents of German residents or citizens. Spouse usually needs to demonstrate A1 German language skills.
requirements: Sponsor has valid German residence permit or citizenship
Genuine family relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate)

citizenship.name: Einbürgerung (Naturalisation)
citizenship.requiresResidency: 5 years legal residence (reduced from 8 under 2024 reform). 3 years for exceptional integration.
citizenship.note: Germany now allows dual citizenship as of June 2024

benefits.healthInsurance: Gesetzliche Krankenversicherung (GKV) mandatory for most employees
benefits.unemployment: Arbeitslosengeld I — up to 60% of net salary for up to 24 months

taxNotes: Germany taxes worldwide income for tax residents. Double taxation agreements with 90+ countries including China, India, Turkey, USA.
